semanticCore: Obtaining, reaching, affecting, getting, or benefit
quranicRelevance: Reaching/affecting someone ('lan yanala Allaha luhoomoha'), and obtaining/getting rewards.

keyDerivations.meaning: To obtain, reach, affect, touch, secure, or acquire

figurativeOrRareUsages: لَنْ يَنَالَ اللَّهَ لُحُومُهَا وَلَا دِمَاؤُهَا وَلَكِنْ يَنَالُهُ التَّقْوَى (22:37): Neither their flesh nor their blood reaches/affects God, but it is devotion from you that reaches Him.
summaryEn: The root refers to obtaining and reaching, focusing on how devotion reaches the divine (rather than physical sacrifices) and the attainment of reward.
summaryAr: يشير هذا الجذر إلى نيل الشيء وإدراكه، واصفاً بلوغ التقوى والنية الصادقة لله عوض اللحوم والدماء الجسدية (يناله التقوى).
rawText: أَنْيَلَ 4 see 1. إِنَانَةٌ The act of giving: (PS in art. نيل;) the giving a gift. (KL.) It seems properly to belong to art. نول. نَالَ مِنْهُ 1 He defamed him. (L, art. قبح.) === نَالَ مِنْ عِرْضِهِ He defamed him. (T, K, TA.) === نَال مِنْهُ He harmed, hurt, or injured him, namely, an enemy. (Mgh.) === نَالَهُ بِلِسَانِهِ is coupled in the Msb, art. قرص, with أَذَاهُ; and seems plainly to signify أَصاَبَهُ, which, in this case, is the same as أَذَاهُ. And 🡻تَنَاوَلَهُ has a similar meaning. === نَالَ He obtained, or attained. (S, K.) نَالَ مِنْ عَدُوِّهِ He attained [or obtained] the object of his aim, or desire, from his enemy. (Msb.) === نَالَهُ, aor. يَنَالُ, It reached him; came to him; syn. وَصَلَ إِلَيْهِ. (M, art. نيل.) See also Bd, xxii. 38. === نَالَ لَكَ أَنْ نَفْعَلَ, aor. يَنِيلُ; and 🡻أَنَالَلَكَ: see أَنَى. === نَالَهُ, first. pers. نِلْتَهُ, inf. n. نَيْلٌ, He obtained it; he attained it; namely, the object of his wish, &c. (S, K, Msb, &c.) نَيْلٌ (T, M, K) and 🡻نَائِلٌ (M, K) What one obtains, or acquires, (T, M, K,) of the bounty of another; like نَوَالَ. (T.) نَيْلٌ Obtainment; &c.: see 1. هُمَا يَتَنَايَلَانِ 6 and signify the same. (TA.)
